name: Boa
description: An embeddable ECMAScript engine written in Rust with 94% Test262 conformance

language-family.comment: Full ECMAScript implementation, aims for latest spec conformance

first-release.comment: Repository created August 2018; crates.io publishing as boa_engine started 2022

paradigm.comment: Supports all JavaScript paradigms including prototype-based OOP

pattern-matching.comment: JavaScript has destructuring but no true pattern matching (TC39 proposal still Stage 1)

error-handling.comment: JavaScript uses try/catch/finally/throw for error handling

async-await.comment: Full async/await support as per ECMAScript specification

iterators-generators.comment: Supports ES6 iterators, generators, and for-of loops

module-system.comment: Supports both ES modules and CommonJS

standard-library.comment: Full ECMAScript built-ins: Object, Array, String, Number, Date, Math, JSON, Promise, etc.

execution-strategy.comment: Lexer, parser, compiler to bytecode, then bytecode interpreter

compilation-model.comment: Can evaluate source directly or parse to AST for repeated execution

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be re-evaluated at runtime

debugging-support.comment: trace and flowgraph features for VM instruction debugging

startup-time.comment: Bytecode interpreter with no JIT warmup overhead

execution-speed.comment: Bytecode VM without JIT; slower than V8/SpiderMonkey but optimized interpreter

memory-overhead.comment: Lighter than full JIT engines like V8; designed for embedding

garbage-collection.comment: Uses Trace and Finalize traits for garbage collection

pure-rust.comment: Entirely written in Rust with no C/C++ dependencies

api-safety.comment: GC requires Trace implementations; some unsafe internally but safe public API

idiomatic-api.comment: Good Context/JsValue API, derive macros for JsData, Result-based error handling

async-compatible.comment: Uses tokio, futures-lite, and futures-concurrency for async job queues

function-registration.comment: NativeFunction wrapper, IntoJsFunctionCopied and UnsafeIntoJsFunction traits

type-mapping.comment: TryIntoJsResult trait for conversions; serde for complex types via deser feature

no-std-compatible.comment: Most dependencies require std; not designed for no_std environments

wasm-compatible.comment: Has WASM playground; wasm-bindgen and web-time dependencies for browser support

blank-slate-mode.comment: Starts with full ECMAScript standard library; cannot easily remove built-ins

memory-sandboxing.comment: JavaScript VM prevents direct host memory access

cpu-limits.comment: No built-in instruction counting or execution time limits found in documentation

memory-limits.comment: No built-in heap size limits found in documentation

syscall-restrictions.comment: No built-in file/network I/O; only what host exposes via native functions

capability-security.comment: Standard JavaScript environment without fine-grained capability controls

default-deny.comment: Full JavaScript standard library available by default

share-data.comment: Data converted through JsValue; no direct references between Rust and JS

custom-types.comment: NativeObject trait and JsData derive macro for exposing Rust types

operator-overloading.comment: JavaScript doesn't support operator overloading; only valueOf/Symbol.toPrimitive for coercion

method-syntax.comment: JsObject supports method definition and dot-notation calls

callbacks-events.comment: JavaScript functions can be passed as callbacks

package-manager.comment: No dedicated package ecosystem; can use ES modules or CommonJS

editor-support.comment: Uses standard JavaScript tooling; VS Code extensions, ESLint LSP work with JS syntax

example-projects.comment: 959 dependent projects on GitHub; Temporal_rs used in V8

documentation-quality.comment: Good API docs with examples; could use more getting-started tutorials

maturity.comment: Self-described as 'experimental'; version 0.x but with 94% Test262 conformance

primary-use-cases.comment: JavaScript embedding for Rust applications; Temporal_rs integrated into V8
